query: what is behaviorism and how does it view learning
notes: c2 is the behaviorist-paradigm explanation — Watson 1913, operant conditioning, programmed instruction, competency-based learning roots. Single-concept.

query: constructivism core principles how learners build knowledge
notes: c9 covers constructivism — Piaget, assimilation/accommodation, Vygotsky ZPD. Single-concept.

query: cognitive load theory working memory limits instructional design
notes: c32 is the canonical CLT explanation (Sweller, working-memory limits). c40 is the online-course application (dense text, visual hierarchy, multi-window navigation). Multi-concept query.

query: cognitive theory of multimedia learning Mayer dual channels
notes: c33 is the direct CTML explanation — Mayer, dual channels, contiguity principle. Single-concept.

query: ADDIE analysis phase needs learner task context analysis
notes: c18 is the ADDIE Analysis-phase deep-dive. c17 gives the Gagne/ISD/ADDIE military roots. Both relevant for a learner asking how ADDIE's analysis phase works.

query: backward design Understanding by Design three stages
notes: c25 is the direct UbD / backward-design explanation — Wiggins & McTighe's three stages (desired results → acceptable evidence → learning experiences). Single-concept.

query: Community of Inquiry teaching presence social presence cognitive presence
notes: c53 covers teaching-presence (design + facilitation + direct instruction). c54 covers 'designing for all three presences' — both needed for a learner asking about the CoI framework in practice.

query: formative assessment vs summative assessment purpose
notes: c60 is formative assessment (Black & Wiliam, muddiest point, peer review). c61 is summative + authentic assessments. Natural pair for this comparative query.

query: Universal Design for Learning multiple means of engagement representation action
notes: c75 is the canonical UDL principles explanation — CAST, three principles with their 'why/what/how' gloss. Single-concept.

query: OSCQR rubric standards areas online course quality review
notes: c81 enumerates the six OSCQR standards areas. c67 covers specifically standards 37-40 (Assessment Alignment and Variety) — good for queries about OSCQR assessment expectations. Multi-concept coverage.

query: scaffolding Vygotsky zone of proximal development
notes: Synonym/adjacency — 'scaffolding' and 'ZPD' are closely linked. c9 covers constructivism with explicit ZPD treatment. Tests the retriever when the query uses the Vygotsky framing rather than bare 'constructivism'.
c10 is the direct connectivism-as-digital-age-theory chunk (Siemens/Downes, MOOC context). Single-concept.

query: expertise reversal effect novices vs experts instructional support
notes: c39 is the expertise-reversal-effect explanation with worked-example-effect and problem-completion fading. Single-concept.

query: how do I design a synchronous online teaching session
notes: Procedural/how-to. c45 is the synchronous-session-design activity. c46 covers affordances and challenges of synchronous learning (social presence, Zoom fatigue, parallel participation). Both needed.

query: POUR principles of web accessibility in course design
notes: c74 is DIGPED's own POUR explanation (Week 11 accessibility module). Cross-course bridging test — shows that DIGPED_101 has accessibility content that should match an accessibility-centric query. Single-concept.
